{{Quote|Scarlet Bloodhawk — quite a colorful name — and probably a colorful character to match.|A Rebel|<ref name="Class" />}}
[[Captain/Legends|Captain]] '''Scarlet Bloodhawk''' was a [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] sympathizer who operated, at different times, a [[Scarlet Bloodhawk's spaceport|repairing port]] for starships and a smuggler starship.

==Biography==
Scarlet Bloodhawk was a [[Gender/Legends|female]] who owned a [[Scarlet Bloodhawk's spaceport|spaceport]] with repair facilities during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], which was staffed by herself, her [[domodroid]] [[H2-1B4]], and the [[Ky'lessan]] known as "[[Snake-Eyes]]" among others. At some point, Snake-Eyes turned on Bloodhawk, locking her up in her own quarters and taking control of the [[Spaceport/Legends|spaceport]]. During this time, tasks such as maintaining the [[Portable beacon|landing beacons]] were neglected.<ref name="Class" />

While Bloodhawk was imprisoned, an old friend of hers<ref name="Class" /> who was a member of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]],<ref name="TRG">[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game (first edition)|''Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game'']] states that all player characters are members of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebellion]].</ref> arrived at the spaceport with their [[Starship/Legends|ship]] and crew needing repairs. While Snake-Eyes pretended to help them, he in fact called in the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] to capture the Rebels in return for a reward. H2-1B4, loyal to Bloodhawk and knowing her old friend as well, relayed Snakes-Eyes' intentions to the group of Rebels. The Rebels broke into Bloodhawk's quarters and freed her. However, during the attempt, the alarm was raised and Scarlet, H2-1BA and the Rebels were forced to flee into the wilderness. With the Empire due to arrive soon, Bloodhawk and the group sneaked into the repair dock where their ship was held and succeeded in taking control of it. However, an Imperial [[Starcutter/Legends|cutter]] was inbound to their position, and the group discovered that the [[Engine/Legends|engines]] of their ship had been sabotaged. Bloodhawk and H2-1B4 worked to repair the ship while the [[Spacer/Legends|spacers]] exchanged fire with Imperial [[Soldier/Legends|troops]] outside. Eventually, the ship was repaired and lifted off, pursued by the cutter. The spacers attempted to engaged the ship's [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]], but found it inoperable.<ref name="Class" />

==Personality and traits==
Scarlet Bloodhawk was known to not spare expenses in important tasks, including maintenance of landing beacons. She was also a trustworthy friend.<ref name="Class" />

==Behind the scenes==
===Non-canon content===
Sometime later, Bloodhawk was [[Captain/Legends|captain]] of the ''[[Capri Nitari]]'', a [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] ship crewed by herself, [[Haynes]], and [[Shandar (smuggler)|Shandar]]. At some point they made a successful [[Kessel Run/Legends|Kessel Run]] and spent the next [[Standard week/Legends|week]] and a half on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Mandrine/Legends|Mandrine]] in and out of local [[Cantina/Legends|bars]]. In [[Maizie's]] while drunk, Bloodhawk stumbled into the booth next to hers and encountered the [[Human/Legends|Human]]–[[Orlak]] hybrid [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenary]], [[Kelly Vermillion]]. The same night, she watched as [[Kronus]] and his heavies entered the bar to start a brawl. Bloodhawk and her crew retreated to a safe distance, watching as a [[Shaliz'Na]] named [[Tyric (Shaliz'Na)|Tyric]], who had been playing the [[Synthesizer/Legends|synthesizer]], drew Kronus' fire to keep the patrons safe. One of Kronus' minions drew a [[Thermal detonator/Legends|thermal detonator]], and was swiftly [[Death/Legends|killed]] by someone else, but Tyric scooped up the thermal detonator and used it to kill Kronus.<ref name="Rogues">{{PolyhedronCite|59|The New Rogues Gallery: Unsung Heroes of the Rebellion}}''</ref>

Bloodhawk also encountered a [[Homanan]] [[Changeling/Legends|shapeshifter]] named [[Nana]] who was part of Vermillion's crew; Nana had previously changed into a [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiee]] and had been nosing around the ''Capri Nitari''. When Bloodhawk confronted Vermillion about the Wookiee, she was surprised to learn it had been Nana all along. Later, Bloodhawk was at a Rebel base when a [[Proliferan]]—an intelligence [[Ant/Legends|ant]]-like creature—known as [[Spy 189|"Red"]] turned up looking for fun and adventure, and hooked up with Vermillion's crew. Bloodhawk kept tabs on the group as they worked for the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]], but vowed to stay clear of them. On another visit to Maizie's, Bloodhawk met with a [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] named [[Justice (bounty hunter)|Justice]] and his "pet" [[Truth]].<ref name="Rogues" />
A known Rebel sympathizer, Bloodhawk was trusted by the Alliance to know the location of one of their bases. She kept a log of her adventures, and was known to like a drink.<ref name="Rogues" />
